item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is not too public, since it is still internal. Announcing the actual punishment serves to deliver the message clearly to the personnel of the site that such a thing will not be accepted. Also, it is one way of giving closure to any personnel involved, or even just accompanying the developments.

Probably you would be right about a message written to the WWS, but internally I do not see any reason not to announce the exact punishment their colleague is going to receive. And to deliver the message to the reader, in the process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[…] to betray the entire purpose of the Foundation by releasing anomalies in the hands of MC&D is clearly grounds for execution or similarly severe punishment.

Yes, but I'm wondering if talking about executions is like talking about sex; everyone knows what you're talking about but no one says it explicitly. This message from Site Director Edgar Holman seems to be written for the whole of Site-64, I think an announcement so public would not so readily talk about formal execution. But that's just a me-thing.

I'll take a look over and see if I agree with you on the size / radius suggestion.

Thanks for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice read, quickly upvoted. A simple anomaly used as proxy to tell a good story about the people that is involved in containment. No overly complex or epic trope needed to be shoved, and none was, save for one of the best uses of Thaumiel I ever saw, one that doesn't even need to be explained to the new reader because of its true purpose in the story.

Excellent, I must say.

For the critique, I do have a few. First the Area of Effect description was awkward at some points. Nothing major, but I would strongly recommend to change the word size for radius every single time it occurred.

The name of the Foundation is more of a matter of personal taste nowadays, but I'm a strong adherent to the idea that the org is just called Foundation, without any SCP in the name. Actually, without a real name to begin with. The motto is unofficial, the item numbers are because something in the containment procedures have to be non-standard (there is a section for anomalies with standard containment), the organization just is.

And finally, it is a rare occurrence for me to find an article where the coldest aspects of the Foundation where downplayed too much, but this is one such a case. In the Foundation's world, compromising an operation to the point of potentially harming the very structure of a containment complex, to betray the entire purpose of the Foundation by releasing anomalies in the hands of MC&D is clearly grounds for execution or similarly severe punishment.

Sweet, sweet victory… this marks my 8th +100. I will obtain 10 >:D

Seriously though, thanks for all the readership. This is also WWS’s 6th +100! So yay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tim Wilson be like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed reading about the issues arising between the Foundation and Wilson's Wildlife Solutions within a skip, particularly given that this skip's anomalous feature is to induce feelings of tranquility. There were one or two things that I would have phrased differently, such as "Investigation onto its origins begins." and "It is believed that over eight other individuals aided Dr. Rodney". Either way, it was a fun examination of the relationship between two organisations with similar goals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now all I can think of is Tim Wilson being a pseudonym for this guy:

Long link collapsed, for the sake and sanity of our mobile readers
hide

https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=5&ved=0ahUKEwj7tO6ju87aAhWLKsAKHW9oAp8QtwIIWjAE&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DaEwZjCm6lks&usg=AOvVaw1p64nKyeE7kA4_6TAcGMP_

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually spelled out in Clef's series "Sequence" IIRC in the one called "Assessment"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stark-Crazy-Pelicans Foundation.
More SCP acronyms
even more
Somehow, more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, what else would it stand for?

Samuel Clemens' Proposed Foundation, duh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, what else would it stand for? "Secure, Contain, Protect" is an unofficial motto, and every contained SCP is named so because it has Special Containment Procedures. Everyone thinks that it sounds weird… which was why I included it in the article. I don't think I've seen it written out anywhere else, and it feels weird, but it is inarguably what it has to stand for. I literally just put it in because I felt like it, despite it feeling weird to read, for this reason.

Special Containment Procedures Foundation.

Yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice article, I'm getting quite fond of Wilson's Wildlife. One nitpick: the line "Special Containment Procedures Foundation" seems really weird to me. Does the SCP in SCP Foundation really stand for that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're really going full force integrating WWS into Site-64 and I find that to be great.

I like the inclusion of MC&D here, as I can think of many of their customers who would want a pet turtle like this to help them just, you know, chill out.

Also, nice AR nod.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I assume that Wilson referring to the Foundation as "you guys" multiple times is a product of his dalliances in amateur pharmacology in the '60s.

I love this, it's like a bright, happy, biological counterpart to AR. Keep Oregon anomalous!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm totally headcanoning Tim Wilson as having a collection cat-themed sweaters and a shopping list that includes "more carrots for the unicorns".

He's also given bellyrubs to multiple anomalies classified as "hostile".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
whoa

i've seen you somewhere before..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww this scips is so heartwarming and i feel bad for the GoI that got bamboozled by the MTF

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hadn't noticed the creation and spread of Wilson's Wildlife, but it's really intriguing. You're redefining what a GoI can be. As far as I can tell, they're not necessarily anomalous in an of themselves, but they work closely with the Foundation, that's their whole purpose, and I don't think I've seen that before. I like it.

Granted, before I started checking the crosslinks, part of me was happy that there was this GoI out there who just wanted to have some turtles. Like, c'mon guys, just let 'em have the happy turtles, it's okay! Still, the story here is fun, mostly because of how petty the conflict is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aha, silly me. That's why I shouldn't be reading these two hours after I need to be asleep..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s the point. It was fabricated so that Ellars would maintain containment of the turtles instead of it being handed to Wilson’s Wildlife Solutions. It’s the final straw, as it’s so clearly him bending the rules for himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I'm missing something, the memetic suppression pops up without warning and promptly disappears forever, like it's just shoved in to qualify it as a thaumiel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I want one of those turtles.

Upvoted without question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know of a couple people with WWS articles in the works, which would definitely put it over the 5 articles and 3 authors required, so yes. New GoI in the future

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A new GOI? A new GOI.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I MADE ART
YAY

This is my second Wilson's Wildlife Solutions SCP! Not much to say about this one, other than I made it and then posted it in under a day. Hopefully that doesn't show too much. I like this one though. I don't expect amazing ratings from it, but a short piece exploring inter-GoI politics was a fun one to write, and I hope that accomplishing this in a concise manner worked.

Here's to the third Wilson's Wildlife article, with a total of two authors. The others, for those wondering, are as of the time of writing: SCP-3466 and SCP-3153. Happy camping!
